'''Dale Ferguson''' (born 13 April 1988) is a former Scottish International professional rugby league footballer who played as {{rlp|LF}} or {{rlp|SR}} 

He previously played in the Super League for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in two separate spells, as well spending time at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. He has also played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. In 2019 he was loaned from the [[Huddersfield Giants|Giants]] to [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He played one game for the [[England Knights]] side in 2012.

==Background==
Ferguson was born in [[Pontefract]], [[Yorkshire, England]].

==Playing career==
On 3 November 2011 The annual [[RLIF Awards]] dinner was held at the [[Tower of London]], and Ferguson was named [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] player of the year.<ref>{{cite news|title=Slater scoops player award|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15313/7289077/Slater-scoops-player-award|access-date=5 November 2011|newspaper=[[Sky Sports]]|date=3 November 2011}}</ref>

In February 2013, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] signed Ferguson on a one-month loan deal. He scored his first try for The Robins in their 44–18 win over the [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] club on 17 February 2013.

===Bradford Bulls===
Midway through the 2013 season the [[Bradford Bulls]] announced that they had signed Ferguson for the 2014 and 2015 seasons on a two-year deal.{{citation needed|date=December 2022}}

====2014====
Ferguson missed the pre-season games against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] due to injury. He did feature in the friendly against [[Castleford Tigers]].

He missed Rounds 1–7 due to injury. Ferguson featured in Round 8 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]) to Round 9 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]) then in Round 15 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). Ferguson was injured for Rounds 16–22 but played in Round 23 against ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]) to Round 25 ([[Castleford Tigers]]). He featured in Round 4 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]) in the [[Challenge Cup]].

Even though the Bradford outfit were relegated and would play in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] in 2015, Ferguson committed himself to Bradford until the end of the 2016 season.{{citation needed|date=December 2022}}

====2015====

Ferguson did not feature in any of the pre-season friendlies.

He featured in Round 3 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) to Round 4 ([[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]]) then in Round 6 ([[Workington Town]]) to Round 7 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). He also featured in Round 9 ([[London Broncos]]) to Round 12 ([[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]). He played in Round 17 against Dewsbury to Round 21 against Sheffield. Ferguson played in Qualifier 1 against ([[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]]) to Qualifier 6 ([[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]). Ferguson played in the £1 Million Game ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Workington Town]]) to Round 5 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]).{{citation needed|date=December 2022}}

====2016====

Ferguson featured in both pre-season friendlies against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and [[Castleford Tigers]].

He featured in Round 1 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) to Round 4 against Leigh then in Round 6 against [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]]. He played in Round 8 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]) to Round 12 ([[London Broncos]]). Ferguson featured in Round 14 [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] to Round 17 ([[Workington Town]]) then in Round 22 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]) to Round 23 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]). He played in the Championship Shield in Game 5 [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] to the Final [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]]. Ferguson played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the 4th against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]].

At the end of the season Ferguson signed a two-year deal with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/10675583/dale-ferguson-rejoins-huddersfield-from-bradford|title=Dale Ferguson rejoins Huddersfield from Bradford|work=Sky Sports|date=28 November 2016|access-date=20 February 2017}}</ref>

===Dewsbury Rams===
On 13 October 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=Dale Ferguson on the move in the Championship|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/dale-ferguson-on-the-move-in-the-championship/}}</ref>

On 1 May 2024 it was reported that, after a disappointing start to the 2024 season, he had stepped down from the head coach role, but would remain in the squad as a player, with assistant coach [[Paul March]] taking over as Head Coach. Fergerson retired from rugby after sucstaing an injury in March 2025. <ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 May 2024|title=Ex-Super League ace resigns as Championship boss – but stays with club as player|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-super-league-ace-resigns-as-championship-boss-but-stays-with-club-as-player}}</ref>

===International career===
Ferguson made his [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] début in 2010, in a defeat by [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]. He was named in the Scotland squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15319/8949738/scotland-name-six-uncapped-players-in-world-cup-squad|title=Scotland name six uncapped players in World Cup squad|date=September 30, 2013|work=Sky Sports}}</ref>

In October and November 2015, Ferguson played in the [[2015 European Cup]].
